title: Arkansas Constitution and Ballot Path
subtitle: A specialized track for deep study of constitutional structure, ballot power, and democratic design.
description: A deep-dive civic scholarship pathway focused on Arkansas constitutional history, ballot systems, representation, and institutional design.
audience: Researchers, lawyers, policy advocates, ballot leaders, constitution scholars.

milestones.title: Historical and Constitutional Frame
milestones.description: Complete the historical and constitutional frame milestone by finishing the required Arkansas civics modules.

course_refs.reason: Ground constitutional study in Arkansas history.
Understand amendment power and the ballot process.

milestones.title: Institutional Design
milestones.description: Complete the institutional design milestone by finishing the required Arkansas civics modules.

course_refs.reason: Study representation systems and structural design choices.
Connect constitutional change to practical campaign execution.

milestones.title: Gatekeeping and Rule Changes
milestones.description: Complete the gatekeeping and rule changes milestone by finishing the required Arkansas civics modules.

course_refs.reason: Analyze backlash, courts, and secretarial gatekeeping.
Think structurally about long-term democratic maintenance.
